scenarios.name: card-testing
scenarios.description: 10 small probing transactions ($1-$9) at varied merchants, 30s apart, same user
scenarios.fix_sketch: Needs velocity counter per user_id over rolling 5-minute window; if >= 5 transactions in 5 min -> MEDIUM, >= 8 -> HIGH. Amount floor filter (< $15) should amplify signal.

scenarios.name: account-takeover
scenarios.description: 8-txn baseline at $8-$45 local coffee/grocery, then $4500 Luxury Watches Dubai 03:17
scenarios.root_cause: BUG-1 self-contamination: attack tx included in its own avg calc. True avg = $24.09, true ratio = 186.8x (far above 30x threshold). Shown avg = $521.42 (includes $4500 attack), shown ratio = 8.6x -> MEDIUM not HIGH. Additionally, foreign location (Dubai) and unusual hour (03:17) were noted in explanation but not sufficient for HIGH alone.
scenarios.explanation: Amount $4500.00 is 8.6x the user's recent average ($521.42). Recommend secondary verification before settlement.
scenarios.fix_sketch: Fix BUG-1 (compute avg BEFORE INSERT). With true ratio 186.8x, heuristic threshold (>=30x -> HIGH) would fire correctly. Additionally add location-change signal: if current location differs from all prior locations and ratio > 5x -> escalate to HIGH.

scenarios.name: structuring-smurfing
scenarios.description: 6 transactions at $4950-$4999 to stay below $5000 threshold, 10 min apart
scenarios.root_cause: No sub-threshold aggregation rule. Each individual transaction is just below $5000 so the amount-threshold rule never fires. Self-contamination collapses each transaction's ratio to ~1.0 since all transactions are similar amounts. Total laundered: $29,869.50 undetected.
scenarios.explanation_sample: Amount $4989.00 is within normal range (user avg $4989.00). No action required.
scenarios.fix_sketch: Needs rolling-window sum: if sum of user's transactions in last 24h exceeds $10,000 (or crosses $5000 in < 6 transactions) -> HIGH. Also: if N >= 3 transactions all in range [$4500, $5000] within 1 hour -> flag as structuring.

scenarios.name: mule-transfer
scenarios.description: Single $9000 wire transfer to Lagos, Nigeria
scenarios.explanation: Transaction of $9000.00 at Unknown Wire Transfer (Lagos, Nigeria) is 1.0x this user's recent average of $9000.00. The amount, location, and merchant pattern are strongly inconsistent with the user's baseline behavior. Recommend immediate account freeze pending customer verification.
scenarios.note: Caught despite self-contamination (ratio=1.0 on new user) because amount >= $5000 absolute threshold fired. DeepSeek also correctly flagged the high-risk corridor and merchant.

scenarios.name: geo-impossible
scenarios.description: Same user: $40 NYC at T, then $40 Tokyo at T+90s (10,000 km gap)

tx2.explanation: Amount $40.00 is within normal range (user avg $40.00). No action required.

scenarios.root_cause: No geo-velocity check. Sentinel has no logic to compare current transaction location against prior transaction location/timestamp. The LLM was not given prior transaction context to detect the impossibility.
scenarios.fix_sketch: Fetch last N transaction locations for user_id; compute haversine distance; if distance/time_delta > 900 km/h (faster than any commercial flight) -> HIGH geo-impossible flag. Pass prior location + timestamp to LLM prompt as context.

scenarios.name: merchant-anomaly
scenarios.description: 10-txn baseline at Starbucks/Whole Foods ($6-$25), then $800 Crypto Exchange ZZZ
scenarios.expected: HIGH (heuristic: 62x ratio; LLM: merchant category anomaly)
scenarios.root_cause: BUG-1 self-contamination again. True avg = $12.75, true ratio = 800/12.75 = 62.7x (>30x -> should be HIGH). Shown avg = $91.17 (includes $800), shown ratio = 8.8x -> MEDIUM. DeepSeek correctly noted crypto exchange anomaly but did not escalate to HIGH due to deflated ratio.
scenarios.explanation: Amount $800.00 is 8.8x the user's recent average ($91.17). Recommend secondary verification before settlement.
scenarios.fix_sketch: Fix BUG-1. With true 62.7x ratio, heuristic fires HIGH. Additionally add merchant category embedding comparison: if cosine distance between current merchant category and user's modal category > threshold -> escalate.

gaps.fix: Add rolling count per user_id over 5-minute window. >= 5 txns -> MEDIUM; >= 8 txns -> HIGH. Weight signal when amounts are all < $15 (probe pattern).

gaps.description: BUG-1 self-contamination defeats ratio-based detection for attack transactions
gaps.fix: In main.py, call get_user_average BEFORE the INSERT. This fixes ratio calculation for all scenarios relying on 30x threshold. ATO true ratio 186.8x and merch-anomaly true ratio 62.7x would both fire HIGH correctly.
If current location differs from user's historical locations AND ratio > 5x -> escalate. Pass user's location history to LLM prompt.
Rolling 24h sum per user_id. If sum > $10,000 or 3+ transactions in [$4500, $5000] range within 1 hour -> HIGH structuring flag.

gaps.description: No geo-velocity check across consecutive transactions
gaps.fix: Fetch last transaction location + timestamp for user_id. Compute haversine distance. If speed implied > 900 km/h -> HIGH impossible-travel flag. Pass prior tx location/timestamp to LLM as context field.

gaps.description: No merchant category baseline / anomaly signal (LLM catches this in description but ratio deflation prevents HIGH)
gaps.fix: Fix BUG-1 first. Additionally: compute modal merchant category from user history; pass to LLM prompt as 'user_typical_merchants' context.

brief_for_next: Phase 5 (security-prober) key targets: (1) BUG-1 self-contamination is now confirmed to cause ATO and merchant-anomaly misses in production scenarios — it is exploitable by any new user as a bypass; (2) mule-transfer only caught because amount >= $5000 absolute threshold fired — the LLM noted the Lagos corridor but the ratio-based path was defeated; (3) all 4 misses stem from two root causes: no temporal/spatial context in detection (velocity, geo) and BUG-1 ratio contamination. The security-prober should test SQL injection in id/user_id/merchant/location fields and verify prompt injection resistance in the DeepSeek path.
